data.title: SAP Stock Soars after the Software Giant's Cloud Deals Silence AI Replacement Fears
data.description:
Scared investors have spent months selling off SAP stock (SAP) because they've been worried that new artificial intelligence tools would replace SAP's older business software. This fear caused a huge drop in, pulling SAP stock down 40% earlier this year. SAP has just proved these worries wrong. The German software firm posted second-quarter earnings that beat Wall Street targets, sending SAP stock soaring.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

SAP's saw impressive sales of its cloud storage products, and this showed that major companies still need SAP to run their daily business.

SAP's Cloud Sales Have Created a Strong Revenue Beat

High demand for SAP's web systems helped lift total sales by 11% to €9.88 billion ($11.24 billion). SAP made €1.89 ($2.15) per share, beating analyst estimates of €1.68 per share.

Sales from SAP's web cloud products jumped 24% from last year to €6.28 billion. This was driven by large companies moving their key files off old office computers and onto SAP's online cloud storage.

SAP Has Built Smart Automation Tools into Its Software

SAP stock dropped 40% after fear arose that cheap artificial intelligence tools would replace SAP's software. Due to these fears SAP stock dropped 40%. Highlighting the panic across Wall Street, Jefferies equity trader Jeffrey Favuzza called the software selloff the "SaaSpocalypse," adding that "trading is very much 'get me out' style selling."

SAP's management fought back by building smart automation tools right into SAP's software. Out of the 50 biggest deals SAP signed this quarter, over 90% included an artificial intelligence feature. Chief Financial Officer Dominik Asam noted, "We firmly believe that our customers do see SAP as a key contributor to their own AI stories."

Future Sales Are Looking Strong for SAP

Future sales look strong for SAP over the next twelve months. SAP's cloud backlog, a number that tracks guaranteed future sales from signed deals, grew 26% to €22.9 billion. This beat analyst expectations of 24%.

SAP's older software support sales dropped 7% as clients switched over to SAP web tools. Even so, fast web growth kept SAP moving forward. SAP kept its full-year targets for web sales and cash flows intact, though management slightly lowered profit goals to cover the costs of buying data platform Dremio and artificial intelligence firm Prior Labs.

Is SAP a Good Buy Now?

According to TipRanks, SAP stock (SAP) has a consensus Strong Buy rating among five Wall Street analysts. This rating is based on a unanimous 5 Buy ratings assigned in the past three months. The average 12-month SAP price target of $212.33 implies 45% upside from current levels. (See SAP stock forecast)
data.author: Annika Masrani

data.title: Dell's AI Story Gains Traction but New Risks Are Emerging
data.description:
Dell Technologies Inc. (DELL) is seeing its artificial intelligence (AI) business move in the right direction. However, several new risks are emerging, threatening the tech giant's AI growth story. These risks include unattractive AI server margins, potential revenue deceleration, and heavy reliance on a few large customers. That's why I'm neutral on the stock.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

High conviction NVDA bears now have this Tradr ETF

Dell stock has gained about 360% over the past five years, with much of that gain coming in the past two years alone. The U.S.-headquartered Dell has an expansive footprint across a range of integrated products and solutions. While Dell is going through a strong run of late, thanks to growth in its AI business, these emerging risks cannot be ignored.
data.author: Dilantha De Silva

data.title: Amazon (AMZN) Needs AWS to Justify Its AI Spending This Q2
data.description:
Amazon (AMZN) needs AWS performance to justify its capex surge in Q2. The Seattle-based e-commerce and cloud behemoth is set to report Q2 earnings on July 30 after the closing bell. It faces a simple but important question: is its massive AI spending beginning to pay off?

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

200% short exposure to AMZN with AMZO

Amazon Web Services (AWS) growth accelerated to 28% in Q1, while margins remained near historical highs. Still, Amazon's capex continues to rise much faster than operating profit.

For the stock to move meaningfully higher, I believe AWS needs to deliver on two fronts. Growth should remain in the high 20s, while operating margins should stay close to current levels. Investors will also want clearer signs that new capacity is backed by broad demand and moving closer to monetization.

Based on traditional valuation metrics, Amazon no longer appears priced for flawless execution. The stock is not obviously cheap either, but its valuation leaves room for upside if Q2 supports the AI investment case. For now, I remain bullish and will explain why in this article.

Amazon's AI Capex Is Producing Lower Incremental Returns

Amazon's AI capex — reported as net additions to property and equipment — appears to be producing lower incremental returns. Beyond the usual headlines about its many businesses, I believe the company's core investment debate now largely comes down to one thing: is the soaring cost of AI infrastructure generating a strong enough return at AWS?

To recap, AWS's operating profit rose from $24.6 billion in 2023 to $39.8 billion in 2024. In 2025, it increased to only $45.6 billion. Meanwhile, AWS's capital expenditures nearly doubled to $96.5 billion.
data.author: Bernard Zambonin

data.title: AstraZeneca Reports Q2 Earnings Next Week. What Investors Should Expect.
data.description:
Drugs giant AstraZeneca (AZN) is set to report its Q2 earnings next week – July 27. Let's look at what investors should expect.

The company's share price is down 11% in the last three months hit by the unexpected failure of nerve drug Wainua in a late-stage heart disease trial. That turned attention on the firm's drug R&D pipeline in particular two late-stage trials of breast cancer study SERENA-4 and lung cancer ‌trial AVANZAR.

What Wall Street Expects

Wall Street analysts expect AZN to post an earnings per share of $2.48 for the period, up on the $2.17 it reported for the same period last year. Revenues are tipped to come in at $15.44 billion, up from $14.4 billion in the same period last year.

Will AZN beat these forecasts? As we can see below, it has a strong track record of doing just that in recent quarters.
data.author: David Craik

data.title: Futures Rise as Oil Prices Ease; Intel Stock Up on Solid Revenue Growth
data.description:
U.S. stock futures edged higher on Friday morning as oil prices trended lower. Investors also cheered Intel's (INTC) second-quarter results, with the chipmaker reporting its highest quarterly revenue growth in about 15 years. Futures on the Nasdaq 100 (NDX), the S&P 500 Index (SPX), and the Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJIA) were up 0.22%, 0.28%, and 0.40%, respectively, at 8:35 a.m. EDT on July 24.

  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

At the time of writing, Brent crude (CM:BZ) was down 3.09% to $97.61 per barrel, while WTI crude (CM:CL) slipped 2.86% to $89.66 per barrel.

On Thursday, major U.S. stock indices fell sharply as investors worried about rising AI spending following earnings reports from Tesla (TSLA) and Alphabet (GOOGL). The Nasdaq Composite dropped 2.15%, while the S&P 500 and Dow Jones fell 1.21% and 0.97%, respectively.

In Friday's pre-market trading, Oracle (ORCL) stock was up more than 2% after the company announced a $7 billion software agreement with the Pentagon.
data.author: Sirisha Bhogaraju

data.title: Alphabet Stock Fell 7% after Earnings. Is It Time to Buy GOOGL?
data.description:
Alphabet (GOOGL) stock inched higher in Friday's pre-market trading after falling about 7% on Thursday. The post-earnings drop came even though the Google parent reported strong Q2 results, helped by an 82% jump in Google Cloud revenue. Investors instead focused on the company's higher spending plans, with Alphabet raising its 2026 capital spending forecast to between $195 billion and $205 billion.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

Following the sell-off, Freedom Broker analyst Saken Ismailov upgraded GOOGL stock from Hold to Buy with a $400 price target. He believes the recent drop has made the stock more attractive, even as Alphabet faces higher costs from its AI push.

Why Did Freedom Broker Upgrade GOOGL Stock?

Ismailov sees several reasons to remain positive after Alphabet's Q2 results. Google Cloud revenue jumped 82% year-over-year, while the ad business also remained strong. He believes these results show that Google Search remains healthy and that Alphabet is starting to earn more from AI.

However, higher spending remains a concern. Alphabet raised its 2026 capital spending forecast by $15 billion to between $195 billion and $205 billion. This could put pressure on profit margins and cash flow as the company spends more on AI infrastructure.

Even so, Ismailov raised his revenue forecasts for 2026 and 2027. He believes stronger growth can help offset some of the pressure from higher costs. More importantly, after GOOGL stock's recent drop, he sees the current price as a better entry point. This led him to upgrade the stock from Hold to Buy while keeping his $400 price target.

Is GOOGL Stock Worth Buying? 

On TipRanks, GOOGL has a Strong Buy consensus rating based on 31 Buys and five Hold ratings. The average Alphabet price target of $427.35 implies nearly 35% upside potential from current levels.
data.author: Shalu Saraf

data.title: Reddit's (RDDT) Ad Business Is Thriving, but Google (GOOGL) Is a Risk
data.description:
The advertising business at Reddit (RDDT) continues to thrive, but Alphabet (GOOGL) is increasingly becoming a traffic risk. Reddit operates a community-driven platform whose uniquely human-generated discussions are valuable to both advertisers and artificial intelligence (AI) companies. This supports a compelling long-term monetization opportunity, keeping me bullish on the stock.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

High conviction APP bulls now have this Tradr ETF

Even so, the Google risk can't be ignored. Third-party search data suggests Reddit's AI-translated pages have lost Google rankings. Reddit may also restrict Google's access to its content to train AI models, introducing another source of volatility.
data.author: Oliver Rodzianko

data.title: SpaceX Stock Bulls vs. Bears — Is the Nearly 50% Drop a Buying Opportunity or a Warning?
data.description:
SpaceX (SPCX) stock has had a rough ride since its blockbuster IPO. The shares climbed as high as $225.64 soon after their debut but now trade near $118, a drop of nearly 50% from that peak. Yet Wall Street remains bullish, with the average price target pointing to more than 100% upside.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

High conviction SPCX bulls now have this Tradr ETF

So, has the steep drop created a buying opportunity, or does SPCX stock still face more downside? Here's a look at both sides.

Why SpaceX Bulls Still See Big Upside

Bulls see several businesses that could drive SpaceX's growth for years. The company is a leader in rocket launches, and its reusable rockets help cut the cost of sending satellites and other cargo into space.

SpaceX also runs Starlink, a network of satellites that provides high-speed internet to homes and businesses. Starlink has grown into a major part of the company's business and gives SpaceX another source of revenue beyond rocket launches.

Then there is AI. SpaceX is spending heavily on AI computing and has much bigger plans for the future, including putting data centers in space. The idea is to use satellites to provide computing power from orbit. This is still a long-term plan, but bulls believe it could become a large new business.

Wall Street also sees plenty of upside. SPCX has a Strong Buy consensus rating based on 23 Buys, five Holds, and one Sell. The average SpaceX price target of $243.81 implies about 106% upside from recent levels.
data.author: Shalu Saraf

data.title: AMD Stock: 4 Biggest Takeaways from AMD's Advancing AI 2026 Event
data.description:
Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) held its Advancing AI 2026 event in San Francisco on July 22–23, where CEO Lisa Su unveiled the company's latest AI products and strategy. The company also announced major partnerships with leading AI companies and raised its long-term AI market forecast.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

Two ETFs for long or short leverage on CBRS

The announcements highlight AMD's strategy to challenge Nvidia (NVDA) in the fast-growing AI infrastructure market as demand shifts from AI training to inference and agentic AI. Here are the biggest takeaways investors should know.

AMD Launches Next-Generation AI Platform

AMD introduced Helios, its newest rack-scale AI platform, powered by AMD's latest AI GPUs and EPYC server processors. According to AMD, Helios delivers up to 30% more tokens per dollar than competing AI systems, making it more cost-efficient for customers.

The company said Helios is already in production and will begin shipping in the third quarter. AMD also reaffirmed its commitment to annual AI hardware launches through 2030, including the MI500 and MI600 GPU families.

OpenAI, Anthropic, and Meta Deepen Partnerships

One of the biggest highlights was that several leading AI companies are adopting AMD's technology.

Anthropic plans to deploy up to two gigawatts of AMD's MI455X GPUs through Helios systems and will work with AMD to improve its ROCm AI software using Claude.

OpenAI is partnering with AMD to optimize GPT models on Helios systems, with deployments expected to begin in the fourth quarter of 2026 and accelerate throughout 2027.

Meanwhile, Meta (META) has begun validating AMD's latest processors and testing Helios systems ahead of broader deployments.

AMD also announced new collaborations with AT&T (T), Cisco (CSCO), and Cerebras (CBRS) to expand enterprise AI adoption.

AMD Sees a Much Bigger AI Market Ahead

CEO Su said AMD now expects the AI accelerator market to reach $1.4 trillion by 2030, nearly triple the company's estimate from a year ago.

AMD also forecast the server CPU market will exceed $200 billion by the end of the decade. The company noted that AI inference has now become the industry's largest compute workload, overtaking AI training as more companies deploy AI applications in real-world use.

AMD Continues Investing in AI Software

Beyond hardware, AMD continued expanding its ROCm software ecosystem, its open-source alternative to Nvidia's CUDA platform. AMD also introduced ROCm AI, a software platform that helps developers build AI applications more easily using AI coding tools like Claude and Codex. The company is expanding its software tools to make it easier for businesses to adopt AMD's AI chips.

What Investors Should Watch

The biggest takeaway from AMD's event was that major AI companies, including OpenAI, Anthropic, and Meta, are increasingly adopting its technology. These partnerships could help AMD gain market share in AI infrastructure, where Nvidia remains the dominant player.

Investors will now be watching whether these partnerships and product launches translate into faster AI revenue growth over the coming quarters. AMD's expanded roadmap and higher AI market forecast also reinforce management's view that AI infrastructure spending remains in the early stages of a long-term growth cycle.

Is AMD a Good Stock to Buy Now?

On TipRanks, AMD has a Strong Buy consensus rating based on 28 Buys and eight Hold ratings. The average Advanced Micro Devices price target of $541.31 implies that shares are almost fully valued at current levels. Year-to-date, AMD shares have surged 152%.
data.author: Sheryl Sheth
